records.ety: [OF. abouter, aboter; cf. F. aboutir, and also abuter; a (L. ad) + OF. boter, buter, to push: cf. F. bout end, and but end, purpose.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Abutted; p. pr. & vb. n. Abutting.]

defs: To project; to terminate or border; to be contiguous; to meet; -- with on, upon, or against; as, his land abuts on the road.
